name: Worm Infestation (Helminthiasis / GI Parasites)
description: Gastrointestinal parasitism by roundworms, tapeworms or flukes. Usually shows as slow production loss. Severe in young calves and during monsoon. Can cause bottle jaw, anaemia and death in heavy burdens.
clinical_signs: slightly elevated heart rate in severe cases
progressive weight loss poor body condition
visible worms in faeces in some cases

diagnostic_criteria.definitive: Faecal Egg Count (FEC) > 500 epg. Worm species identification.

treatment_info.veterinary_treatment: Strategic anthelmintic (dewormer) treatment. Vet selects product based on local resistance patterns.
supportive_care: Rotational grazing to break parasite cycle
treatment_info.first_aid: Isolate if severely affected. Provide high-quality feed. Contact vet for deworming protocol.
treatment_info.do_not: Do not deworm repeatedly with same drug – resistance develops. Test before treating.
